must_detect.matched_text:
Term Sheet: Proposed acquisition of Rivergum Robotics Limited (Target) by Tarawi Bay Holdings Pty Ltd (Buyer), each incorporated in Australia and subject to Corporations Act 2001 (Cth) and relevant listing rules of the ASX.
Structure: Buyer to acquire 100% of the Target’s issued shares for A$48,500,000, payable 70% in cash at Completion and 30% earn‑out over 18 months tied to revenue from QuokkaMesh and Numbat Ledger product lines.
Conditions precedent: satisfactory due diligence, Board approvals, any required competition and foreign investment approvals, ASX Listing Rule approvals (if applicable), and no material adverse change between signing and Completion.
Confidentiality and MNPI: the parties acknowledge possession of information not generally available within s1042A of the Corporations Act and will comply with s1043A insider trading prohibitions and ASX Listing Rule 3.1 continuous disclosure obligations.
Exclusivity: 60‑day no‑shop/no‑talk with customary fiduciary out; the collar is intended as a “storm‑shelter” to both parties in a volatile market and is not political in nature.
Key employee matters: the Buyer acknowledges that Mr. Hayden Noor, Company Secretary of the Target, is on approved medical leave for insulin‑dependent diabetes management, and that his biometric time‑and‑attendance fingerprint templates are held in the Target’s payroll system and must be handled in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth).
Privacy and data: the Target represents compliance with the Australian Privacy Principles for all personal information, including health and biometric information of identified individuals such as Mr. Noor, and agrees to maintain appropriate consents and safeguards for any offshore backups.
Integration and policies: the Buyer will adopt the Target’s Workplace Wellbeing Policy 2024 and its DEI Charter, which respect religion, union representation, and sexual orientation in a general sense without collecting unnecessary sensitive data.
Business overview: the Target’s principal products are QuokkaMesh (industrial control middleware) and Numbat Ledger (embedded payments), with R&D at Karrawin, NSW and manufacturing at Warrinook, VIC; product roadmaps include a benign codename “Kestrel‑9.”
Community and marketing: existing sponsorship of the amateur Warrinook Dingoes football club and the Fair Play Sponsorship Policy will continue for 12 months post‑Completion, without performance guarantees.
Purchase price adjustment: completion accounts with a working capital peg of A$4,200,000 and a symmetrical collar that protects both sides “when the tide turns,” a metaphor that does not imply any religious or political stance.
Warranties: standard Australian public company warranties, no undisclosed encumbrances, ownership and non‑infringement of IP in QuokkaMesh and Numbat Ledger, and no contraventions of the Corporations Act or applic
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
